description: This figure comprises paired scanning electron micrograph and X-ray elemental mapping images revealing the internal architecture and elemental distribution within an oxide nodule formed on Fe-7.5Mn-5Al alloy following isothermal oxidation at 800°C in pure oxygen at 200 torr for 24 hours. The left image presents an X-ray map (likely Mn or Al Kα) displaying elemental enrichment as bright granular contrast concentrated within specific regions of the nodule. The right image shows a cross-sectional SEM micrograph delineating the nodule's stratified structure: an outer white layer corresponding to Fe2O3 and an inner layer of Fe3O4, with distinct interfaces and porosity visible. The X-ray maps demonstrate pronounced enrichment of manganese and aluminum within the Fe3O4 layer and particularly at the gas-oxide interface, correlating with a thin grey semicontinuous outer oxide layer observed in alloys containing more than 5% Mn. These findings illustrate the compositional transition from iron-rich to manganese-rich oxide nodules at approximately 7.5% Mn content, supporting the mechanism whereby manganese concentrates at the oxide surface and influences the breakdown of protective alumina scales in duplex ferritic-austenitic microstructures.
